VolunteerProfile.title: VolunteerProfile
VolunteerProfile.description: Represents a volunteer user, storing their personal details, interests, and skills.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VolunteerProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the volunteer.

email.description: The email address of the volunteer.

contactNumber.description: The contact phone number of the volunteer.

city.description: The city where the volunteer is located.

state.description: The state or region where the volunteer is located.

country.description: The country where the volunteer is located.

bio.description: A short biographical description of the volunteer, highlighting their motivations and experience.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the volunteer's profile picture.

availabilityDescription.description: A textual description of the volunteer's general availability (e.g., 'Weekends only', 'Flexible afternoons').

interestIds.description: References to Interest entities that match the volunteer's interests. (Relationship: Interest N:N VolunteerProfile)

skillIds.description: References to Skill entities that the volunteer possesses. (Relationship: Skill N:N VolunteerProfile)

preferredCauseIds.description: References to Cause entities that represent the volunteer's preferred causes. (Relationship: Cause N:N VolunteerProfile)

registeredAt.description: Timestamp when the volunteer profile was created.

NGOProfile.title: NGOProfile
NGOProfile.description: Represents a Non-Governmental Organization (NGO) on the platform, detailing its mission and activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NGOProfile entity.

organizationName.description: The official name of the NGO.

description.description: A detailed description of the NGO's mission, vision, and impact stories.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the NGO.

contactNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the NGO.

websiteUrl.description: The official website URL of the NGO.

logoUrl.description: URL to the NGO's logo image.

addressStreet.description: The street address of the NGO's main office.

addressCity.description: The city of the NGO's main office.

addressState.description: The state or region of the NGO's main office.

addressCountry.description: The country of the NGO's main office.

foundedDate.description: The date when the NGO was officially founded.

causeIds.description: References to Cause entities that the NGO primarily works on. (Relationship: Cause N:N NGOProfile)

registeredAt.description: Timestamp when the NGO profile was created on the platform.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a volunteering event or project posted by an NGO.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

ngoId.description: Reference to the NGOProfile that posted this event. (Relationship: NGOProfile 1:N Event)

title.description: The title of the event or project.

description.description: A detailed description of the event, including its objectives and activities.

startDate.description: The start date and time of the event.

endDate.description: The end date and time of the event.

locationName.description: A descriptive name for the event's location (e.g., 'Community Hall A', 'Puducherry Botanical Garden').

locationAddressStreet.description: The street address of the event location.

locationAddressCity.description: The city of the event location.

locationAddressState.description: The state or region of the event location.

locationAddressCountry.description: The country of the event location.

requiredSkillIds.description: References to Skill entities required for volunteers participating in this event. (Relationship: Skill N:N Event)

primaryCauseId.description: Reference to the primary Cause entity associated with this event. (Relationship: Cause 1:N Event)

maxVolunteers.description: The maximum number of volunteers required for this event.

status.description: The current status of the event (e.g., 'Open', 'Full', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

postedAt.description: Timestamp when the event was posted.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific skill that volunteers may possess or events may require.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'Web Development', 'First Aid', 'Tutoring').

description.description: A brief description of the skill.

Interest.title: Interest
Interest.description: Represents a volunteer's area of interest for social welfare activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Interest entity.

name.description: The name of the interest (e.g., 'Children''s Education', 'Animal Welfare').

description.description: A brief description of the interest.

Cause.title: Cause
Cause.description: Represents a social welfare cause or category.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cause entity.

description.description: A brief description of the cause.

VolunteerEnrollment.title: VolunteerEnrollment
VolunteerEnrollment.description: Tracks a volunteer's application or commitment to a specific event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VolunteerEnrollment entity.

volunteerId.description: Reference to the VolunteerProfile who enrolled in the event. (Relationship: VolunteerProfile 1:N VolunteerEnrollment)

eventId.description: Reference to the Event the volunteer enrolled in. (Relationship: Event 1:N VolunteerEnrollment)

applicationDate.description: The date and time when the volunteer applied for the event.

status.description: The current status of the volunteer's enrollment (e.g., 'Applied', 'Accepted', 'Rejected', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'CancelledByVolunteer', 'CancelledByNGO').

hoursLogged.description: The number of hours the volunteer logged for this event.

ngoFeedback.description: Feedback provided by the NGO about the volunteer's participation.

volunteerFeedback.description: Feedback provided by the volunteer about the event and NGO.

Certificate.title: Certificate
Certificate.description: Represents a certificate awarded to a volunteer for their participation or achievement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Certificate entity.

volunteerId.description: Reference to the VolunteerProfile who received the certificate. (Relationship: VolunteerProfile 1:N Certificate)

eventId.description: Reference to the Event for which the certificate was awarded (optional). (Relationship: Event 1:N Certificate)

title.description: The title of the certificate (e.g., 'Certificate of Appreciation', 'Volunteer Service Award').

description.description: A description of the achievement or service recognized by the certificate.

issueDate.description: The date when the certificate was issued.

issuerName.description: The name of the entity that issued the certificate (e.g., an NGO or 'ConnectSphere').

certificateUrl.description: URL to a digital copy of the certificate.

ForumTopic.title: ForumTopic
ForumTopic.description: Represents a main discussion topic in the community forum.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ForumTopic entity.

title.description: The title of the forum topic.

description.description: A brief description or opening statement for the topic.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the forum topic was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the forum topic was last updated.

createdByVolunteerId.description: Reference to the VolunteerProfile who created this forum topic. (Relationship: VolunteerProfile 1:N ForumTopic)

ForumPost.title: ForumPost
ForumPost.description: Represents an individual post or reply within a forum topic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ForumPost entity.

topicId.description: Reference to the ForumTopic this post belongs to. (Relationship: ForumTopic 1:N ForumPost)

parentPostId.description: Reference to the parent ForumPost if this is a reply. (Relationship: ForumPost 1:N ForumPost)

content.description: The content of the forum post.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the forum post was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the forum post was last updated.

createdByVolunteerId.description: Reference to the VolunteerProfile who created this forum post. (Relationship: VolunteerProfile 1:N ForumPost)

definition.description: Stores the complete, private profile data for each volunteer. The document ID {volunteerId} must correspond to the Firebase Authentication UID of the volunteer. This structure ensures path-based ownership, making authorization rules for profile management straightforward and highly secure (only the owner can read/write their profile).

params.name: volunteerId
params.description: The unique ID of the volunteer, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores the complete, private profile data for each NGO. The document ID {ngoId} must correspond to the Firebase Authentication UID of the NGO. This structure provides path-based ownership, securing NGO profile management (only the owner can read/write their profile).

params.name: ngoId
params.description: The unique ID of the NGO, corresponding to its Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A denormalized collection containing a publicly visible subset of VolunteerProfile data. This collection supports discovery and matching functionalities (QAPs) without exposing private volunteer information. It is read-only for clients; updates are managed by backend services to synchronize with /volunteer_profiles/{volunteerId}.

params.name: volunteerId
params.description: The unique ID of the volunteer.

definition.description: A denormalized collection containing a publicly visible subset of NGOProfile data. This collection enables public discovery and matching (QAPs) for volunteers. It is read-only for clients; updates are managed by backend services to synchronize with /ngo_profiles/{ngoId}.

params.name: ngoId
params.description: The unique ID of the NGO.

definition.description: A global, publicly readable collection of skills. Used as reference data for both volunteers (to list their skills) and NGOs (to specify required skills for events). Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique ID of the skill.

definition.description: A global, publicly readable collection of interests. Used by volunteers to indicate their areas of interest. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: interestId
params.description: The unique ID of the interest.

definition.description: A global, publicly readable collection of social causes. Used by NGOs to categorize their work and by volunteers to specify preferred causes. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: causeId
params.description: The unique ID of the cause.

definition.description: Publicly viewable collection of events posted by NGOs. Each event document includes the ngoId for authorization independence, allowing direct checks in security rules (e.g., request.auth.uid == resource.data.ngoId) without fetching parent NGO data. All users can read; only the ngoId owner can create, update, or delete their specific events.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID of the event.

definition.description: A top-level collection tracking volunteer applications and commitments to events. Each document includes denormalized volunteerId and ngoId (from the associated Event) to ensure Authorization Independence. This allows both the enrolling volunteer and the event-owning NGO to manage their respective parts of the enrollment directly via rules, facilitating secure QAPs for both volunteer and NGO dashboards.

params.name: enrollmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the volunteer enrollment record.

definition.description: Stores certificates awarded to volunteers. Each document includes the volunteerId for owner-based read access. For authorization independence during creation by an NGO, the document *must also include an issuerNgoId field* (denormalized from the NGO that issued it, if applicable), allowing rules to verify the issuer without get() calls.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: The unique ID of the certificate.

definition.description: Publicly viewable collection of forum topics. Each topic document includes createdByVolunteerId for owner-based write authorization. All users can read; only the creator can update/delete their topics.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the forum topic.

definition.description: Subcollection of posts within a forum topic. Each post document includes createdByVolunteerId and topicId for authorization independence. All users can read; only the creator can update/delete their posts, and topicId in the path must match resource.data.topicId for data integrity.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent forum topic.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique ID of the forum post.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for 'ConnectSphere' prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the mandated design principles, particularly Authorization Independence and support for QAPs (Query As Permissions).

Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) via Denormalization:
To eliminate hierarchical authorization dependencies and avoid expensive/non-atomic get() calls in security rules, key authorization context is denormalized directly into the relevant documents:
*   events/{eventId}: Each Event document explicitly contains the ngoId of its creating NGO. This allows rules to verify ownership directly (e.g., resource.data.ngoId == request.auth.uid) when an NGO attempts to modify their event, without needing to check a parent collection or perform a get() call.
*   volunteer_enrollments/{enrollmentId}: This collection stores enrollment records. Crucially, each VolunteerEnrollment document includes both the volunteerId (the enrolling volunteer's UID) and the ngoId (the UID of the NGO owning the associated event). This denormalization means both the volunteer and the NGO can manage the enrollment record (e.g., volunteer cancels, NGO accepts/logs hours) with rules checking resource.data.volunteerId == request.auth.uid or resource.data.ngoId == request.auth.uid, ensuring atomic operations and clear permissions.
*   certificates/{certificateId}: For certificates awarded to volunteers, each Certificate document contains the volunteerId. Furthermore, to allow an issuing NGO to create certificates without a get() call, the document *must also include an issuerNgoId field* (denormalized from the NGO). This enables direct rule checks like resource.data.issuerNgoId == request.auth.uid for NGO-initiated actions.
*   forum_topics/{topicId} and forum_topics/{topicId}/posts/{postId}: Both ForumTopic and ForumPost documents embed createdByVolunteerId. This field explicitly identifies the owner, allowing simple resource.data.createdByVolunteerId == request.auth.uid checks for modification rights. For posts, topicId is also included, simplifying data integrity checks.

QAPs (Query As Permissions) Support through Structural Segregation and Dedicated Collections:
The structure is designed to enable secure list operations, ensuring that clients only receive data they are authorized to see, minimizing the need for server-side filtering and preventing accidental data exposure. This is achieved primarily through structural segregation:
*   Public vs. Private Profiles: Instead of a single profile collection, we separate:
    *   /volunteer_profiles/{volunteerId} and /ngo_profiles/{ngoId}: These store complete, private profile data, accessible only by the respective owner (request.auth.uid).
    *   /public_volunteer_listings/{volunteerId} and /public_ngo_listings/{ngoId}: These dedicated, top-level collections contain *only* publicly viewable subsets of profile information. They are read-only for all users (including anonymous) and are populated/updated by backend services to synchronize with the private profiles. This allows for broad search, filtering, and matching functionalities (list operations) without compromising private data.
*   Global, Public Reference Data: Collections like /skills, /interests, and /causes are globally readable by all users (authenticated or anonymous). This allows all clients to fetch necessary reference data for UI rendering and filtering without authorization complexities. Write access is restricted to administrators (managed via an admin_roles/{uid} collection or similar backend logic).
*   Public Events: The /events/{eventId} collection is designed for public readability by all users, supporting the 'Discovery & Learning' feature's search and filter capabilities directly. Write access is restricted to the owning NGO, enforced by the ngoId field within each event document.
*   User-Specific Data Views: For volunteer_enrollments and certificates, the denormalized volunteerId and ngoId fields allow for efficient, permission-based queries. Volunteers can query volunteer_enrollments where volunteerId == request.auth.uid, and NGOs can query where ngoId == request.auth.uid. Similarly for certificates, volunteers can list certificates where volunteerId == request.auth.uid. This ensures that list operations naturally filter to authorized data subsets.
*   Community Forum: /forum_topics/{topicId} and its subcollection /forum_topics/{topicId}/posts/{postId} are publicly readable, facilitating community browsing and engagement, while write access is strictly limited to the createdByVolunteerId owner.

This comprehensive approach leverages denormalization for atomic operations and authorization independence, and structural segregation for clear, secure QAPs, resulting in a robust and maintainable Firestore design.
